title: Arrest of women — restrictions on time and procedure
text: Section 46 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973 places specific restrictions on the arrest of women. Under Section 46(4), no woman shall be arrested after sunset and before sunrise. Where a female accused is to be arrested after sunset and before sunrise, the police officer shall obtain prior permission from a judicial magistrate of the first class within whose local jurisdiction the offence is committed or the arrest is to be made. This permission must be obtained before making the arrest. The police officer making the arrest of a woman must be a woman officer wherever possible. The arrest of a woman shall be made with strict regard to her dignity. Where a female accused is to be searched, the search shall be made by another female with strict regard to decency. These protections apply regardless of the nature of the offence for which the woman is being arrested.
